summary: postcss-minify-selector-parser typosquats postcss-selector-parser and executes an embedded encrypted payload
details: Package name impersonates the widely-used `postcss-selector-parser` and re-exports its API via `src/selector-parser.js` (`module.exports = require('postcss-selector-parser')`) as cover. The package additionally ships `src/config/defaults.js` containing `DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT` — an AES-GCM ciphertext (iv:tag:data base64 blob) — plus a hardcoded passphrase `default-dev-passphrase` and salt `encode-npm-c-salt`. `src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js` decrypts that blob and passes the cleartext to `new Function('require', runnable)(require)`, executing arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node.js privileges and `require` access. The decode-and-execute path is reachable via `runDefaultDecodedFunction`/`run`, and `runtime/lib.min.js` calls `require('../src').run()`. The package's `scripts.start`, `scripts.test`, and `scripts.decode` all invoke `node runtime/lib.min.js`, so a developer who installs this package as a `postcss-selector-parser` lookalike and runs `npm test` (or any of the documented scripts) executes the encrypted payload. The cleartext is unverifiable without running the decryption, but the structure (opaque ciphertext + hardcoded key + `new Function` sink) is a remote/embedded code-execution primitive with no legitimate reason to exist in a CSS selector parser.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
